content.body: I'm assuming it's because the plan it created roughly ordered recent things by when they were added to the language
Its PLAN.md was apparently generated “from the ECMAScript spec and test262 submodules”, so I wonder how that ordering happened. Not that it matters much.
content.formatted_body: Its PLAN.md was apparently generated “from the ECMAScript spec and test262 submodules”, so I wonder how that ordering happened. Not that it matters much.

content.body: I wonder if it has garbage collection
if I were given the task to implement a spec-compliant JS engine with no further qualifications I definitely would not add garbage collection

> Claude picked a reasonable feature order, but it made some architectural decisions early on (like how to handle generators, **how to structure the GC,** how to deal with WTF-8 strings) that caused expensive rework later.
content.formatted_body: Claude picked a reasonable feature order, but it made some architectural decisions early on (like how to handle generators, how to structure the GC, how to deal with WTF-8 strings) that caused expensive rework later.

content.body: was the answer for how to structure the GC: "use a noop"
